heading: Authorization of appropriations; restrictions; reports to congressional committees
label: 1943 Authorization of appropriations; restrictions; reports to congressional committees
text: In order to carry out the purposes of , there is authorized to be appropriated to the President not to exceed $680,000,000, which shall remain available until expended, and which the President may use, subject to such further legislative provisions as may be enacted, in addition to other funds available for such purposes, on such terms and conditions as he may specify: , That none of the funds made available pursuant to this section shall be used to furnish assistance to any country in Latin America being subjected to economic or diplomatic sanctions by the Organization of American States. The Secretary of State shall keep the Committee on Foreign Relations of the Senate and the Committee on Foreign Affairs of the House currently informed about plans and programs for the utilization of such funds.section 1942 of this titleProvided
notes: CodificationThis section was not enacted as part of the Mutual Security Act of 1954 which comprises this chapter.

Delegation of FunctionsFor delegation of certain functions of President under this section, see Memorandum of President of the United States, , 66 F.R. 30629, set out as a note under .May 30, 2001section 1942 of this title
